name: Libertex
description: Libertex is a Cyprus based broker founded in 1997. With CySEC, it offers 50+ FX pairs, CFDs on stocks, indices, commodities, crypto, ETFs trading primarily via MT4, MT5, Libertex Platform.
fullReview:
# Libertex — Comprehensive Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026  
**Target:** Libertex (libertex.com)  
**Sources:** Wikipedia (Libertex), CySEC (164/12), FCA (829106), BaFin (124498), FSCA, Finance Magnates, CBN Cyprus, TrustPilot (Dec 2025–Apr 2026), batch3 data

---

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Libertex is an online brokerage platform operated by **Indication Investments Ltd**, a Cyprus-based financial services company. The **Libertex brand was established in 2012**, though the parent organization has been active in financial markets since **1997**, previously operating under different brands including **ForexClub** — one of the earliest retail forex brands in the CIS region.

Headquartered in **Limassol, Cyprus**, Libertex has grown into a globally active multi-regulated CFD and forex broker. With the parent organization's history dating to 1997, Libertex benefits from nearly **three decades of market experience** in retail trading, making it one of the longest-tenured brands in the online brokerage industry.

The company operates a **spread-only pricing model** — no separate ECN/raw accounts, no explicit commissions on trades. This simplifies the fee structure for clients but means spread costs are built into every trade.

### Corporate Structure

| Entity | Role | Jurisdiction |
|--------|------|--------------|
| Libertex Group | Parent organization | International |
| Indication Investments Ltd | Operating entity (CySEC) | Limassol, Cyprus |
| Libertex UK Ltd | FCA-authorized entity | United Kingdom |
| MAEX Limited | Offshore brand (LBX) | Mauritius |

### Leadership

| Executive | Role | Background |
|-----------|------|------------|
| **Michael Geiger** | CEO | Joined 2011; previously Ernst & Young Stuttgart |
| **Marios Chailis** | CMO | Since Oct 2019; ex-24option, ex-Henyep Capital Markets |

### Employee Count

**700+ employees** across multiple jurisdictions (Cyprus, UK, Germany, South Africa, and other locations).

### Key Milestones Timeline

| Year | Milestone |
|------|-----------|
| 1997 | Parent organization begins operating in financial markets (ForexClub brand) |
| **2012** | **Libertex brand established** by Indication Investments Ltd |
| ~2013 | CySEC authorization (license 164/12) |
| ~2015–2018 | Mobile app development and platform modernization |
| ~2016–2018 | FCA authorization (829106) |
| ~2018–2020 | BaFin authorization (124498) + FSCA South Africa |
| **2020** | **MT4/MT5 availability** launched alongside proprietary platform |
| **2020 (Sept)** | **Tottenham Hotspur sponsorship** — Official Online Trading Partner |
| **2021 (Aug)** | **CySEC partial license suspension** (compliance concerns); lifted late August 2021 |
| 2021 (Jul) | MT5 offered to European clients |
| 2021–2023 | Expanded instrument range to 300+ |
| **2022 (Aug)** | **FC Bayern Munich sponsorship** announced |
| 2023 | Tottenham Hotspur partnership concluded |
| **2024** | **Libertex Invest launched** — commission-free stock investing |
| **2025 (Feb)** | **F1 sponsorship** — Official Online Trading Partner of KICK Sauber F1 Team |
| **2025 (Aug)** | **LBX offshore brand launched** — MAEX Limited (Mauritius) |

### Office Locations

| Location | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| Limassol, Cyprus | Global headquarters, CySEC entity |
| London, UK | FCA entity |
| Germany | BaFin entity (cross-border) |
| South Africa | FSCA entity |

---

## 2. REGULATION — MULTI-JURISDICTION LICENSING

| Regulator | License | Brand | Tier | Retail Leverage | Key Details |
|-----------|---------|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
| **CySEC** | **164/12** | Libertex | Tier 1 | 1:30 | Primary license; EEA cross-border passporting |
| **FCA** | **829106** | Libertex UK | Tier 1 | 1:30 | UK operations |
| **BaFin** | **124498** | Libertex Germany | Tier 1 | 1:30 | Cross-border activities in Germany |
| **FSCA** | Licensed | Libertex SA | Tier 2 | Up to 1:600 | South African operations |

### Key Regulatory Details

**CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission)** — License 164/12 is the primary authorization, permitting Libertex to provide cross-border services throughout the European Economic Area and Switzerland under MiFID II.

**FCA (UK)** — Reference 829106 authorizes Libertex to serve UK clients with FSCS protection of £85,000.

**BaFin (Germany)** — Reference 124498 for cross-border activities in the German market.

**FSCA (South Africa)** — Licensed for South African operations with higher leverage limits (up to 1:600).

### Regulatory Actions

⚠️ **CySEC Partial Suspension (August 2021):** In August 2021, CySEC **partially suspended** the license of Indication Investments Ltd, citing concerns about the company's compliance procedures. Libertex stated it was operating in full compliance, and the **suspension was lifted in late August 2021** — well ahead of the September 17th deadline imposed by CySEC. The quick resolution suggests the issues were procedural rather than fundamental.

### Regulatory Standing (Current)

Libertex currently maintains a **clean regulatory standing** across all four jurisdictions. No active enforcement actions, fines, or restrictions are in place as of April 2026.

### Negative Balance Protection

✅ **Yes** — Provided under CySEC, FCA, and BaFin regulations. Clients cannot lose more than their account balance on CFD trades.

### Investor Compensation

| Entity | Scheme | Coverage |
|--------|--------|----------|
| CySEC (164/12) | ICF (Cyprus) | €20,000 |
| FCA (829106) | FSCS (UK) | £85,000 |
| BaFin (124498) | Via CySEC ICF | €20,000 |
| FSCA | No statutory scheme | N/A |

---

## 3. TRADING CONDITIONS — COMPLETE BREAKDOWN

### 3.1 Account Types

| Account Type | Spreads | Commission | Min Deposit | Best For |
|-------------|---------|-----------|-------------|----------|
| **Standard (Libertex)** | From 1.0 pip | $0 | $10 | Beginners, casual traders |
| **Libertex Invest** | N/A | $0 | Varies | Long-term investors (stocks) |

**Libertex Invest (2024):** A new account type allowing users to **purchase and hold actual stocks** without commission fees — a significant departure from CFD-only trading. This positions Libertex to compete with commission-free stock brokers like Trading 212 and eToro.

### 3.2 Spread-Only Model — Detailed

Libertex operates a **spread-only model** across all instruments:

| Instrument | Typical Spread | Details |
|-----------|---------------|---------|
| EUR/USD | 1.0–1.5 pips | Major pair |
| GBP/USD | 1.5–2.0 pips | Major pair |
| USD/JPY | 1.2–1.8 pips | Major pair |
| Gold (XAU/USD) | 30–50 cents | Commodity |
| Oil (WTI) | 3–5 cents | Commodity |
| S&P 500 | 0.5–1.0 points | Index |
| EUR/GBP | 1.5–2.0 pips | Cross pair |
| Bitcoin | 30–60 USD | Crypto CFD |

**Note:** Spreads are variable and widen during low-liquidity periods, news events, and market closures. The "from 1.0 pip" on EUR/USD is the minimum; average spreads are typically 1.0–1.5 pips.

### 3.3 Instruments — 300+

| Category | Count | Details |
|----------|-------|---------|
| Forex | 30+ pairs | Major, minor, exotic pairs |
| Commodities | 20+ | Metals, energies, agriculture |
| Indices | 15+ | Global stock indices |
| Share CFDs | 200+ | US and European stocks |
| Crypto CFDs | 10+ | Bitcoin, Ethereum, altcoins |
| ETFs | 15+ | Commodity and index ETFs |
| Stocks (Invest) | Growing | Actual stocks via Libertex Invest |

### 3.4 Leverage by Entity

| Entity | Forex | Indices | Commodities | Stocks | Crypto |
|--------|-------|---------|-------------|--------|--------|
| CySEC (EU) | 1:30 | 1:20 | 1:10–1:20 | 1:5 | 1:2 |
| FCA (UK) | 1:30 | 1:20 | 1:10–1:20 | 1:5 | 1:2 |
| BaFin (Germany) | 1:30 | 1:20 | 1:10–1:20 | 1:5 | 1:2 |
| FSCA (South Africa) | Up to 1:600 | Up to 1:200 | Up to 1:100 | Up to 1:20 | Up to 1:10 |

### 3.5 Order Types

| Order Type | Available |
|-----------|-----------|
| Market | ✅ |
| Limit | ✅ |
| Stop | ✅ |
| Trailing Stop | ✅ |
| OCO | ❌ |
| Bracket Orders | ❌ |
| Iceberg | ❌ |

---

## 4. PLATFORMS — COMPREHENSIVE

### 4.1 Libertex Proprietary Platform

Libertex's proprietary platform is designed for **simplicity and accessibility**:

**Web Platform:**
- Clean, intuitive interface
- Charting with multiple timeframes (1M to 1W)
- Technical indicators (Moving Averages, RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands)
- Portfolio overview with P&L tracking
- One-click trading
- Economic calendar integration
- Market news feed

**Mobile App (iOS/Android):**
- Full trading functionality
- Push notifications for alerts and execution
- Biometric login (fingerprint/Face ID)
- Positive reviews for reliability and ease of use
- "Trustworthy and secure" (TrustPilot user feedback)
- Some criticism of "overpacked" interface on Android

### 4.2 MetaTrader 4 (MT4)

✅ **Available** — Standard MT4 access for EU/UK clients:
- Full Expert Advisor (EA) support
- Custom indicators
- Backtesting capability
- MQL4 programming language
- Copy trading via MT4 signals

### 4.3 MetaTrader 5 (MT5)

✅ **Available** (since July 2021) — Extended instrument coverage:
- More order types than MT4
- Economic calendar built-in
- MQL5 programming language
- Depth of market (DOM)
- Multi-asset support

### 4.4 Platform Comparison

| Feature | Libertex Platform | MT4 | MT5 |
|---------|------------------|-----|-----|
| Ease of Use | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐ |
| Charting | ⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Algo Trading | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Copy Trading | ⚠️ Limited | ✅ Signals | ✅ Signals |
| Mobile | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Custom Indicators | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |

### 4.5 What's Missing

| Feature | Available? |
|---------|-----------|
| cTrader | ❌ |
| TradingView integration | ❌ |
| Proprietary API | ❌ |
| Social/copy trading (native) | ❌ (MT4/MT5 signals only) |
| VPS hosting | ❌ |
| FIX API | ❌ |

---

## 5. FEES & COSTS — FULL BREAKDOWN

### Trading Costs

| Cost Type | Amount | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| Spread (EUR/USD) | From 1.0 pip | Average 1.0–1.5 |
| Commission | $0 | All instruments |
| Swap/Overnight | Variable | Applied to positions held overnight |
| Deposit | $0 | All methods |
| Withdrawal | $0 | Standard methods |
| Inactivity | May apply | After extended inactivity |
| Currency conversion | Spread-based | Applied when trading in non-account currency |

### Swap Rates

Overnight financing charges (swaps) apply to all CFD positions held past the daily rollover time (typically 22:00 GMT). Swap rates vary by instrument and are published on the Libertex website. Triple swaps are charged on Wednesdays (to account for weekend financing).

### Deposit & Withdrawal

| Method | Deposit Time | Withdrawal Time | Fee |
|--------|-------------|----------------|-----|
| Credit/Debit Card | Instant | 1–5 business days | $0 |
| Bank Wire | 1–3 business days | 1–3 business days | $0 |
| E-wallets (Skrill, Neteller) | Instant | 1–2 business days | $0 |

**Base Currencies:** EUR, USD, GBP  
**Minimum Deposit:** $10 (Standard account)  
**Minimum Withdrawal:** Varies by method

---

## 6. USER REVIEWS & REPUTATION

### TrustPilot Analysis (Dec 2025–Apr 2026)

Libertex maintains an active TrustPilot presence with detailed review engagement.

### Positive Reviews

✅ **"Reliable platform with professional support...executions are stable, and customer support responds quickly."** (January 26, 2026)  
✅ **"Fast and helpful support — Dagmar helped me quickly with verification."** (January 20, 2026)  
✅ **"Libertex is trustworthy and secure, I like the variety of instruments."** (December 29, 2025)  
✅ **"Easy to handle. Trustworthy."** (January 2, 2026)  

### Negative Reviews

❌ **"The general framework is not practical and overpacked. Transparency is not practical."** (February 11, 2026) — Android app criticism  
❌ **Recovery service spam** — Fake "recovery service" reviews common on all financial platforms  
❌ **Spread widening** — Some users report wider spreads during volatility  

### Review Response Quality

Libertex demonstrates **excellent review response practices**:
- Detailed, thoughtful responses to genuine complaints
- Active engagement with positive reviews
- Clear messaging against recovery service scams: "We do not cooperate with third party recovery services"

### Community Reputation

Libertex is generally well-regarded in the CFD trading community. The 29-year parent history and multi-regulator licensing build trust. The CySEC suspension in 2021 (quickly resolved) is the only notable regulatory blemish.

---

## 7. COMPETITIVE POSITION — CFD Broker Landscape

### Head-to-Head Comparisons

| Feature | Libertex | Axi | eToro | Plus500 | XTB |
|---------|---------|-----|-------|---------|-----|
| Founded | 1997 (parent) | 2007 | 2007 | 2008 | 2002 |
| Regulators | CySEC/FCA/BaFin/FSCA | ASIC/FCA/DFSA/FSA | CySEC/FCA/ASIC | FCA/CySEC/ASIC | FCA/CySEC |
| Pricing | Spread-only | Spread+ECN | Spread-only | Spread-only | Spread-only |
| Platforms | Prop+MT4/MT5 | MT4/MT5 | Proprietary | Proprietary | xStation5 |
| Instruments | 300+ | 220+ | 3,000+ | 2,800+ | 5,800+ |
| Min Deposit | $10 | $0 | $10–$200 | $100 | $0 |
| Copy Trading | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Stock Investing | ✅ Invest | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |

### Libertex's Competitive Advantages

1. **29 years parent history** — one of the longest-tenured brands in retail trading
2. **4 strong regulatory licenses** — CySEC, FCA, BaFin, FSCA
3. **MT4 + MT5 + proprietary** — platform flexibility
4. **Libertex Invest** — commission-free stock investing (new)
5. **$10 minimum deposit** — extremely accessible
6. **Major sports sponsorships** — brand visibility (Tottenham, Bayern, F1)
7. **Spread-only simplicity** — no hidden commission structures
8. **Clean regulatory record** — one minor suspension, quickly resolved

### Libertex's Competitive Weaknesses

1. **Spreads not the tightest** — 1.0–1.5 EUR/USD vs. 0.0–0.3 at ECN brokers
2. **No cTrader or TradingView** — excludes traders who prefer these platforms
3. **Limited instrument range** — 300+ vs. 3,000–5,000+ at competitors
4. **No native copy trading** — only MT4/MT5 signals
5. **Private ownership** — no public financial transparency
6. **No ECN accounts** — no raw spread option
7. **Limited algo support** — MT4/MT5 EAs only, no FIX API
8. **Offshore brand (LBX)** — August 2025 launch may confuse brand identity

### Positioning Summary

Libertex occupies the **"established, multi-regulated CFD broker for intermediate traders"** niche. It's well-suited for traders who want reliable MT4/MT5 access with a solid regulatory framework and don't need ultra-tight ECN spreads or the broadest instrument selection. The addition of Libertex Invest (commission-free stocks) signals a strategic pivot toward broader investment services.

---

## 8. SPONSORSHIPS & BRAND PARTNERSHIPS

Libertex has invested heavily in high-profile sports sponsorships:

### Tottenham Hotspur (2020–2023)

- **Role:** Official Online Trading Partner
- **Duration:** 3 years (September 2020 to 2023)
- **Exposure:** Premier League — one of the world's most-watched football leagues
- **Significance:** Massive brand visibility across global audiences

### FC Bayern Munich (2022–present)

- **Role:** Partner
- **Duration:** Announced August 2022
- **Exposure:** Bundesliga and Champions League
- **Significance:** Partnership with one of Europe's most successful clubs

### KICK Sauber F1 Team / Audi Revolut F1 Team (2025–present)

- **Role:** Official Online Trading Partner
- **Duration:** Announced February 2025
- **Exposure:** Formula 1 — global motorsport audience
- **Significance:** Entry into the high-profile world of F1 sponsorship

### Sponsorship Strategy Analysis

Libertex's sponsorship portfolio spans **football (Premier League, Bundesliga)** and **motorsport (Formula 1)**, targeting:
- European and global audiences
- High-income demographics
- Male-skewed audiences who overlap with trading demographics
- Brand building in key European markets (UK, Germany)

This level of sponsorship investment suggests significant marketing budgets and a commitment to brand growth.

---

## 9. LIBERTEX INVEST — COMMISSION-FREE STOCK INVESTING

### Overview

Launched in **2024**, **Libertex Invest** is a significant product expansion allowing users to **purchase and hold actual stocks** — not CFDs — without commission fees.

### Key Features

- **Actual stock ownership** — not CFDs, you own the underlying shares
- **$0 commission** — no trading fees on stock purchases
- **Long-term holding** — designed for buy-and-hold investors
- **Dividends** — receive actual dividend payments
- **No overnight fees** — because you own the shares, no swap charges

### Strategic Significance

Libertex Invest represents a **fundamental shift** in Libertex's positioning:
- From **pure CFD broker** → **multi-product investment platform**
- Competes directly with DEGIRO, Trading 212, and eToro for stock investors
- Addresses the criticism that CFD-only platforms don't serve long-term investors
- May attract a new customer segment that avoids leveraged products

---

## 10. LBX — OFFSHORE BRAND (AUGUST 2025)

### Overview

In **August 2025**, Libertex Group launched **LBX**, a new offshore CFD brokerage brand operated by **MAEX Limited**, a company registered in **Mauritius**.

### Key Details

| Detail | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Brand | LBX |
| Operator | MAEX Limited |
| Jurisdiction | Mauritius (offshore) |
| Parent | Libertex Group |
| Launch | August 2025 |
| Purpose | Higher leverage, different regulatory framework |

### Implications

- **Higher leverage available** — offshore entities can offer 1:500+ vs. 1:30 EU maximum
- **Different regulatory standards** — Mauritius FSC vs. CySEC/FCA
- **Risk factor** — offshore regulation provides weaker investor protection
- **Brand confusion** — may muddy Libertex's "well-regulated" positioning
- **F1 deal connection** — LBX launch coincided with F1 sponsorship announcement

### Risk Assessment

The LBX offshore brand introduces a **moderate risk factor**. While the core Libertex brand remains well-regulated (CySEC/FCA/BaFin/FSCA), the offshore expansion suggests the group is pursuing higher-risk, higher-leverage markets. Investors should understand which entity they're trading with.

---

## 12. PROMOTIONS & INCENTIVES

### Current Promotions

| Promotion | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| Demo Account | Free, unlimited, virtual funds |
| Training Sessions | Platform-specific training offered |
| Libertex Invest | Commission-free stock investing |

### Deposit Bonuses

⚠️ **Not available** for CySEC/FCA-regulated clients — EU/UK regulations prohibit deposit bonuses for retail CFD traders.

### Referral Program

Libertex does not prominently disclose a permanent referral program for EU/UK clients.

---

## 13. AFFILIATE / IB PROGRAM

### Status: AVAILABLE

Libertex operates an **IB (Introducing Broker) program**:

- Available for partners seeking to introduce clients
- Specific commission rates not publicly disclosed (contact Libertex for details)
- Standard IB structure typical of CySEC-regulated brokers
- Revenue sharing based on introduced client trading volume

### Compared to Forex Broker Affiliates

Libertex's IB program is less transparent than competitors who publish CPA ($200–$1,000+) or revenue share (20–50%) rates publicly. Interested partners should contact Libertex directly.

---

## 14. RISK ASSESSMENT — FULL ANALYSIS

### Positive Risk Factors

| Factor | Assessment | Impact |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| 4 strong regulators | ✅ CySEC/FCA/BaFin/FSCA | Low Risk |
| 29 years parent history | ✅ Extremely long track record | Low Risk |
| 700+ employees | ✅ Significant operational scale | Low Risk |
| Major sponsorships | ✅ Tottenham, Bayern, F1 | Low Risk |
| Negative balance protection | ✅ EU/UK mandatory | Low Risk |
| Clean current record | ✅ No active enforcement actions | Low Risk |
| MT4/MT5 availability | ✅ Industry-standard platforms | Low Risk |
| Libertex Invest | ✅ Stock ownership expansion | Low Risk |

### Negative Risk Factors

| Factor | Assessment | Impact |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| CySEC suspension (2021) | ⚠️ Brief partial suspension (resolved quickly) | Low-Medium Risk |
| Private ownership | ⚠️ No public financial transparency | Medium Risk |
| Offshore brand (LBX) | ⚠️ May dilute regulated brand trust | Medium Risk |
| Spread-only model | ⚠️ No transparency on execution quality | Low Risk |
| Limited instrument range | ⚠️ 300+ vs. thousands at competitors | Low Risk |
| No ECN accounts | ⚠️ Cannot verify interbank execution | Low Risk |

### Overall Risk Rating: **LOW-MEDIUM**

Libertex is a well-regulated, established CFD broker with a strong multi-jurisdiction license portfolio. The 29-year parent history, 700+ employees, and major sports sponsorships demonstrate operational scale and commitment. The main risks are: (1) private ownership limiting financial transparency, (2) the 2021 CySEC suspension (though quickly resolved), and (3) the offshore LBX brand expansion.

---

## 16. AWARDS & RECOGNITION

Libertex has received various industry recognitions over its 29-year history, though no specific major awards were identified in this research. The broker's credibility is primarily established through:
- Multi-regulator licensing (4 jurisdictions)
- Long operating history (29 years parent)
- Major sports sponsorships
- 700+ employee scale

---

## 17. FINAL SCORING

### Detailed Scoring

| Category | Score | Max | Notes |
|----------|-------|-----|-------|
| **Regulation** | 8 | 10 | 4 strong regulators; minus for 2021 CySEC suspension |
| **Trading Conditions** | 6 | 10 | Spread-only 1.0–1.5; no ECN; limited instruments |
| **Platforms** | 7 | 10 | Proprietary + MT4/MT5; no cTrader/TradingView |
| **Deposits/Withdrawals** | 7 | 10 | Standard methods, $10 min deposit, $0 fees |
| **Customer Support** | 7 | 10 | Responsive per TrustPilot reviews |
| **Promotions** | 5 | 10 | Demo account, training; no deposit bonuses |
| **Education** | 5 | 10 | Basic training, demo account |
| **Awards** | 4 | 10 | Some recognition; no major identified awards |
| **Brand/Sponsorships** | 8 | 10 | Tottenham, Bayern, F1 — exceptional brand investment |
| **Financial Stability** | 6 | 10 | Private — no public financials; but large scale |

### Trader Type Ratings

| Trader Type | Rating | Verdict |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| Beginners | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | $10 min deposit, simple platform, demo account |
| EU/UK Traders | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Strong regulation, NBP, ICF/FSCS |
| MT4/MT5 Users | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Both platforms available |
| Long-Term CFD Holders | ⭐⭐⭐ | Spreads OK; swaps apply |
| Scalpers/Day Traders | ⭐⭐⭐ | Spreads OK but not tightest; no ECN |
| Stock Investors | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Libertex Invest — commission-free stocks |
| Algo Traders | ⭐⭐ | MT4/MT5 EAs only; no FIX API |
| Copy Traders | ⭐⭐ | Only MT4/MT5 signals |
| High-Leverage (EU) | ⭐ | 1:30 max under CySEC/FCA |
| High-Leverage (Offshore) | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | LBX offers higher leverage |

### Overall Assessment

**Libertex is a well-established, multi-regulated CFD broker with 29 years of parent history and major sports sponsorships (Tottenham, Bayern Munich, F1).** The spread-only pricing model from 1.0 pips is competitive for beginners, and the $10 minimum deposit makes it highly accessible. The 2024 launch of Libertex Invest (commission-free stock investing) and the MT4/MT5 availability broaden its appeal.

However, spreads are not the tightest (1.0–1.5 EUR/USD vs. 0.0–0.3 at ECN brokers), there's no cTrader or TradingView integration, and the private ownership means no public financial transparency. The CySEC suspension in 2021 (quickly resolved) and the 2025 offshore LBX brand expansion are moderate concerns.

For beginners and intermediate traders seeking a regulated, accessible CFD broker with a long track record and brand credibility, Libertex is a solid choice. For professional traders needing ultra-tight spreads, ECN execution, or advanced platform features, better options exist.

---

*Sources: Wikipedia (Libertex), Finance Magnates, CBN Cyprus Business News, CySEC register (164/12), FCA register (829106), BaFin register (124498), TrustPilot (Dec 2025–Apr 2026), batch3 data.*

*Character count: ~20,800+*



---

# Libertex — Deep Dive Analysis

## Company Overview
- **Founded:** 2012 (brand launched by Indication Investments Ltd, parent active since 1997 as ForexClub)
- **Headquarters:** Limassol, Cyprus
- **Parent Company:** Libertex Group (formerly Forex Club Group)
- **CEO:** Michael Geiger (joined 2011, formerly Ernst & Young Stuttgart)
- **CMO:** Marios Chailis (since October 2019, formerly 24option, Henyep Capital Markets)
- **Employees:** 700+
- **Type:** Private Company
- **Group History:** Operating under ForexClub brand since 1997 in Russia/CIS; rebranded to Libertex in 2012 for international markets
- **Offshore Launch:** LBX (MAEX Limited, Mauritius) launched August 2025 for offshore CFD brokerage

## Regulation & Licensing
Libertex operates under a well-regulated European framework with additional offshore presence:

### Primary — Cyprus (European Union)
- **Entity:** Indication Investments Ltd
- **Regulator:** Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC)
- **License Number:** 164/12
- **Authorization Date:** 2012
- **Framework:** MiFID II / Markets in Financial Instruments Directive (MiFID)
- **Services:** Cross-border services throughout EEA and Switzerland
- **BaFin Registration:** Registered with Germany's Federal Financial Supervisory Authority for cross-border activities

### Historical Regulatory Note
- **August 2021:** CySEC partially suspended License 164/12 citing compliance concerns
- **Resolution:** Libertex addressed concerns and suspension was lifted in late August 2021
- **Deadline Met:** Well ahead of September 17, 2021 deadline imposed by CySEC
- **Current Status:** License fully active and operational

### Offshore — Mauritius (New — 2025)
- **Entity:** MAEX Limited (operating as LBX brand)
- **Jurisdiction:** Mauritius
- **Regulator:** Financial Services Commission (FSC) Mauritius
- **License:** Investment Dealer License (pending/active)

- **Purpose:** Offshore CFD brokerage for non-EU markets

- **Minimum Deposit:** $10

### Libertex Invest (Stocks)
- **Minimum Deposit:** $100

## Pros & Cons

### Pros
1. **Strong CySEC Regulation:** Licensed under CySEC 164/12 with EEA cross-border authorization
2. **27+ Years Operating History:** Parent company active since 1997 (as ForexClub)
3. **Libertex Invest:** Innovative $0 commission stock investing alongside CFDs
4. **High Leverage Available:** Up to 1:500 for non-EU clients
5. **Low Minimum Deposit:** Starting from $10 (Cent account)
6. **700+ Employees:** Substantial operational infrastructure
7. **High-Profile Sponsors:** FC Bayern Munich, F1 (Kick Sauber/Audi Revolut), formerly Tottenham
8. **MT4 and MT5 Support:** Full MetaTrader ecosystem available
9. **No Hidden Fees:** Transparent fee structure with $0 commissions on most accounts
10. **Negative Balance Protection:** Standard for all retail clients

### Cons
1. **CySEC Suspension History:** License partially suspended in August 2021 (resolved)
2. **Limited Instruments:** 300+ is modest compared to competitors (NAGA 4,000+)
3. **Crypto-Focused Brand vs Product:** Marketing emphasizes crypto but limited crypto offerings
4. **EU Leverage Cap:** 1:30 for EU retail clients (standard ESMA rules)
5. **Country Restrictions:** Not available in US, UK, Canada, several others
6. **No Copy Trading:** Lacks built-in social/copy trading features
7. **Limited Advanced Analysis:** Less sophisticated research tools than top-tier brokers
8. **Offshore Entity Risk:** New Mauritius LBX brand introduces regulatory uncertainty

## Risk Assessment

**Risk Level: LOW-MEDIUM**

|--------|-----------|
| **Regulatory Quality** | High — CySEC regulation with EEA cross-border authorization |
| **Corporate History** | High — Operating since 1997 with 700+ employees |
| **Segregated Accounts** | Yes — Client funds held in segregated accounts per CySEC requirements |
| **Investor Protection** | High — Access to EU Investor Compensation Fund (up to €20,000) |
| **Financial Stability** | High — Established company with multiple revenue streams |
| **Withdrawal Reliability** | Generally Positive — Standard processing times |
| **Negative Balance Protection** | Yes |
| **Compliance History** | Medium — 2021 CySEC suspension (resolved) |

### Regulatory Jurisdiction Comparison
| Entity | Regulator | License | Compensation | Leverage |
|--------|-----------|---------|-------------|----------|
| Indication Investments Ltd | CySEC | 164/12 | ICF up to €20,000 | 1:30 retail |
| MAEX Limited (LBX) | FSC Mauritius | TBD | Not guaranteed | 1:500 |

## Unique Features

1. **Libertex Invest ($0 Stock Trading):** Commission-free real stock investing with fractional shares — unique combination with CFD platform
2. **27-Year Heritage:** One of the longest-operating retail forex brands (since 1997 as ForexClub)
3. **Dual Platform Strategy:** Proprietary web platform for beginners + MetaTrader for advanced traders
4. **High-Profile Sponsorships:** Global brand visibility through FC Bayern, F1, Tottenham partnerships
5. **Cent Accounts:** Micro-trading option with cent lots for low-risk strategy testing
6. **Full EA Support:** MetaTrader integration allows algorithmic trading strategies
7. **Multi-Brand Strategy:** Libertex (EU/global) + LBX (offshore) + ForexClub (CIS/Russia heritage)
8. **Mobile-First Approach:** Robust mobile app with full trading capabilities
9. **Zero Commission Model:** $0 commissions on most CFD accounts (spread-based revenue)
10. **MiFID II Compliance:** Full European regulatory framework for transparency and investor protection

## Target Traders

### Ideal Client Profile
- **Experience Level:** Beginner to intermediate traders
- **Trading Style:** Casual investors, swing traders, long-term stock investors
- **Geographic Focus:** Europe, CIS/Russia (legacy), Asia, Latin America
- **Capital Range:** $10-$5,000+
- **Primary Interest:** Forex CFDs, commission-free stocks, crypto
- **Platform Preference:** Simple web-based platforms with MetaTrader option
- **Investment Goal:** Combined CFD trading and stock investing in one platform

### Less Suitable For
- High-frequency institutional traders
- Traders seeking extensive asset ranges (4,000+)
- Copy/social trading enthusiasts
- Professional algorithmic traders needing API access
- Traders in heavily restricted jurisdictions (US, UK, Canada)

## Competitive Positioning

### vs. Plus500
| Aspect | Libertex | Plus500 |
|--------|----------|---------|
| Regulation | CySEC 164/12 | CySEC, FCA, ASIC |
| Platforms | Web, MT4, MT5 | Proprietary only |
| Stock Investing | ✅ Real stocks (Libertex Invest) | ❌ CFDs only |
| Min Deposit | $10 | $100 |
| Instruments | 300+ | 2,800+ |
| Verdict | Better platforms, stock investing | More instruments, better regulated |

### vs. IQ Option
| Aspect | Libertex | IQ Option |
|--------|----------|-----------|
| Min Deposit | $10 | $10 |
| Platforms | Web, MT4, MT5 | Proprietary |
| Instruments | 300+ | 300+ |
| Company Age | 27 years | 12 years |
| Regulation | CySEC 164/12 | CySEC 247/14 |
| Verdict | Stronger heritage, better platform options | Similar product range |

### vs. FBS
| Aspect | Libertex | FBS |
|--------|----------|-----|
| Regulation | CySEC 164/12 | CySEC, IFSC, ASIC |
| Max Leverage | 1:500 | 1:3000 |
| Min Deposit | $10 | $5 |
| Stock Investing | ✅ Real stocks | ❌ CFDs only |
| Verdict | Better stock offering, stronger EU presence | Higher leverage, more global |

### Market Position
- **Tier Position:** Mid-tier regulated broker
- **Differentiator:** Combined CFD + commission-free real stock investing
- **Weakness:** 2021 CySEC suspension history, limited instrument range
- **Opportunity:** Growing demand for hybrid CFD/investing platforms

## Investment Considerations

### Suitability Rating
| Criteria | Rating (1-5) |
|----------|-------------|
| Safety & Regulation | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5) — CySEC regulated with EEA authorization |
| Trading Conditions | ⭐⭐⭐ (3/5) |
| Platform Quality | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5) — Dual platform strategy |
| Education | ⭐⭐⭐ (3/5) |
| Customer Support | ⭐⭐⭐ (3/5) |
| Value for Money | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5) — $0 stock investing is exceptional |

### Final Assessment
Libertex offers a compelling combination of CySEC-regulated CFD trading and innovative commission-free stock investing (Libertex Invest). The 27-year operating history (since 1997 as ForexClub) provides institutional credibility that many competitors lack. The 2021 CySEC suspension is a minor blemish that was resolved quickly. With 700+ employees and high-profile sponsorships (FC Bayern, F1), Libertex has substantial operational backing. The platform selection (proprietary web + MT4 + MT5) caters to both beginners and experienced traders. However, the 300+ instrument range is modest, and the new offshore LBX brand introduces some regulatory complexity. Best suited for retail traders and investors in Europe and emerging markets who want a regulated broker with both CFD trading and real stock investing capabilities in one account.

---

# Libertex — Comprehensive Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026  
**Target:** Libertex (libertex.com)  
**Sources:** Wikipedia (Libertex), CySEC (164/12), FCA (829106), BaFin (124498), FSCA, Finance Magnates, CBN Cyprus, TrustPilot (Dec 2025–Apr 2026), batch3 data

---

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Libertex is an online brokerage platform operated by **Indication Investments Ltd**, a Cyprus-based financial services company. The **Libertex brand was established in 2012**, though the parent organization has been active in financial markets since **1997**, previously operating under different brands including **ForexClub** — one of the earliest retail forex brands in the CIS region.

Headquartered in **Limassol, Cyprus**, Libertex has grown into a globally active multi-regulated CFD and forex broker. With the parent organization's history dating to 1997, Libertex benefits from nearly **three decades of market experience** in retail trading, making it one of the longest-tenured brands in the online brokerage industry.

The company operates a **spread-only pricing model** — no separate ECN/raw accounts, no explicit commissions on trades. This simplifies the fee structure for clients but means spread costs are built into every trade.

### Corporate Structure

| Entity | Role | Jurisdiction |
|--------|------|--------------|
| Libertex Group | Parent organization | International |
| Indication Investments Ltd | Operating entity (CySEC) | Limassol, Cyprus |
| Libertex UK Ltd | FCA-authorized entity | United Kingdom |
| MAEX Limited | Offshore brand (LBX) | Mauritius |

### Leadership

| Executive | Role | Background |
|-----------|------|------------|
| **Michael Geiger** | CEO | Joined 2011; previously Ernst & Young Stuttgart |
| **Marios Chailis** | CMO | Since Oct 2019; ex-24option, ex-Henyep Capital Markets |

### Employee Count

**700+ employees** across multiple jurisdictions (Cyprus, UK, Germany, South Africa, and other locations).

### Key Milestones Timeline

| Year | Milestone |
|------|-----------|
| 1997 | Parent organization begins operating in financial markets (ForexClub brand) |
| **2012** | **Libertex brand established** by Indication Investments Ltd |
| ~2013 | CySEC authorization (license 164/12) |
| ~2015–2018 | Mobile app development and platform modernization |
| ~2016–2018 | FCA authorization (829106) |
| ~2018–2020 | BaFin authorization (124498) + FSCA South Africa |
| **2020** | **MT4/MT5 availability** launched alongside proprietary platform |
| **2020 (Sept)** | **Tottenham Hotspur sponsorship** — Official Online Trading Partner |
| **2021 (Aug)** | **CySEC partial license suspension** (compliance concerns); lifted late August 2021 |
| 2021 (Jul) | MT5 offered to European clients |
| 2021–2023 | Expanded instrument range to 300+ |
| **2022 (Aug)** | **FC Bayern Munich sponsorship** announced |
| 2023 | Tottenham Hotspur partnership concluded |
| **2024** | **Libertex Invest launched** — commission-free stock investing |
| **2025 (Feb)** | **F1 sponsorship** — Official Online Trading Partner of KICK Sauber F1 Team |
| **2025 (Aug)** | **LBX offshore brand launched** — MAEX Limited (Mauritius) |

### Office Locations

| Location | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| Limassol, Cyprus | Global headquarters, CySEC entity |
| London, UK | FCA entity |
| Germany | BaFin entity (cross-border) |
| South Africa | FSCA entity |

## 2. REGULATION — MULTI-JURISDICTION LICENSING

| Regulator | License | Brand | Tier | Retail Leverage | Key Details |
|-----------|---------|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
| **CySEC** | **164/12** | Libertex | Tier 1 | 1:30 | Primary license; EEA cross-border passporting |
| **FCA** | **829106** | Libertex UK | Tier 1 | 1:30 | UK operations |
| **BaFin** | **124498** | Libertex Germany | Tier 1 | 1:30 | Cross-border activities in Germany |
| **FSCA** | Licensed | Libertex SA | Tier 2 | Up to 1:600 | South African operations |

### Key Regulatory Details

**CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission)** — License 164/12 is the primary authorization, permitting Libertex to provide cross-border services throughout the European Economic Area and Switzerland under MiFID II.

**FCA (UK)** — Reference 829106 authorizes Libertex to serve UK clients with FSCS protection of £85,000.

**BaFin (Germany)** — Reference 124498 for cross-border activities in the German market.

**FSCA (South Africa)** — Licensed for South African operations with higher leverage limits (up to 1:600).

### Regulatory Actions

⚠️ **CySEC Partial Suspension (August 2021):** In August 2021, CySEC **partially suspended** the license of Indication Investments Ltd, citing concerns about the company's compliance procedures. Libertex stated it was operating in full compliance, and the **suspension was lifted in late August 2021** — well ahead of the September 17th deadline imposed by CySEC. The quick resolution suggests the issues were procedural rather than fundamental.

### Regulatory Standing (Current)

Libertex currently maintains a **clean regulatory standing** across all four jurisdictions. No active enforcement actions, fines, or restrictions are in place as of April 2026.

### Negative Balance Protection

✅ **Yes** — Provided under CySEC, FCA, and BaFin regulations. Clients cannot lose more than their account balance on CFD trades.

### Investor Compensation

| Entity | Scheme | Coverage |
|--------|--------|----------|
| CySEC (164/12) | ICF (Cyprus) | €20,000 |
| FCA (829106) | FSCS (UK) | £85,000 |
| BaFin (124498) | Via CySEC ICF | €20,000 |
| FSCA | No statutory scheme | N/A |

**Libertex Invest (2024):** A new account type allowing users to **purchase and hold actual stocks** without commission fees — a significant departure from CFD-only trading. This positions Libertex to compete with commission-free stock brokers like Trading 212 and eToro.

### 3.2 Spread-Only Model — Detailed

Libertex operates a **spread-only model** across all instruments:

| EUR/GBP | 1.5–2.0 pips | Cross pair |
| Bitcoin | 30–60 USD | Crypto CFD |

**Note:** Spreads are variable and widen during low-liquidity periods, news events, and market closures. The "from 1.0 pip" on EUR/USD is the minimum; average spreads are typically 1.0–1.5 pips.

## 5. FEES & COSTS — FULL BREAKDOWN

### Trading Costs

| Cost Type | Amount | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| Spread (EUR/USD) | From 1.0 pip | Average 1.0–1.5 |
| Commission | $0 | All instruments |
| Swap/Overnight | Variable | Applied to positions held overnight |
| Deposit | $0 | All methods |
| Withdrawal | $0 | Standard methods |
| Inactivity | May apply | After extended inactivity |
| Currency conversion | Spread-based | Applied when trading in non-account currency |

### Swap Rates

Overnight financing charges (swaps) apply to all CFD positions held past the daily rollover time (typically 22:00 GMT). Swap rates vary by instrument and are published on the Libertex website. Triple swaps are charged on Wednesdays (to account for weekend financing).

### Deposit & Withdrawal

| Method | Deposit Time | Withdrawal Time | Fee |
|--------|-------------|----------------|-----|
| Credit/Debit Card | Instant | 1–5 business days | $0 |
| Bank Wire | 1–3 business days | 1–3 business days | $0 |
| E-wallets (Skrill, Neteller) | Instant | 1–2 business days | $0 |

## 7. COMPETITIVE POSITION — CFD Broker Landscape

### Head-to-Head Comparisons

| Feature | Libertex | Axi | eToro | Plus500 | XTB |
|---------|---------|-----|-------|---------|-----|
| Founded | 1997 (parent) | 2007 | 2007 | 2008 | 2002 |
| Regulators | CySEC/FCA/BaFin/FSCA | ASIC/FCA/DFSA/FSA | CySEC/FCA/ASIC | FCA/CySEC/ASIC | FCA/CySEC |
| Pricing | Spread-only | Spread+ECN | Spread-only | Spread-only | Spread-only |
| Platforms | Prop+MT4/MT5 | MT4/MT5 | Proprietary | Proprietary | xStation5 |
| Instruments | 300+ | 220+ | 3,000+ | 2,800+ | 5,800+ |
| Min Deposit | $10 | $0 | $10–$200 | $100 | $0 |
| Copy Trading | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Stock Investing | ✅ Invest | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |

### Libertex's Competitive Advantages

1. **29 years parent history** — one of the longest-tenured brands in retail trading
2. **4 strong regulatory licenses** — CySEC, FCA, BaFin, FSCA
3. **MT4 + MT5 + proprietary** — platform flexibility
4. **Libertex Invest** — commission-free stock investing (new)
5. **$10 minimum deposit** — extremely accessible
6. **Major sports sponsorships** — brand visibility (Tottenham, Bayern, F1)
7. **Spread-only simplicity** — no hidden commission structures
8. **Clean regulatory record** — one minor suspension, quickly resolved

### Libertex's Competitive Weaknesses

1. **Spreads not the tightest** — 1.0–1.5 EUR/USD vs. 0.0–0.3 at ECN brokers
2. **No cTrader or TradingView** — excludes traders who prefer these platforms
3. **Limited instrument range** — 300+ vs. 3,000–5,000+ at competitors
4. **No native copy trading** — only MT4/MT5 signals
5. **Private ownership** — no public financial transparency
6. **No ECN accounts** — no raw spread option
7. **Limited algo support** — MT4/MT5 EAs only, no FIX API
8. **Offshore brand (LBX)** — August 2025 launch may confuse brand identity

### Positioning Summary

Libertex occupies the **"established, multi-regulated CFD broker for intermediate traders"** niche. It's well-suited for traders who want reliable MT4/MT5 access with a solid regulatory framework and don't need ultra-tight ECN spreads or the broadest instrument selection. The addition of Libertex Invest (commission-free stocks) signals a strategic pivot toward broader investment services.

---

## 8. SPONSORSHIPS & BRAND PARTNERSHIPS

Libertex has invested heavily in high-profile sports sponsorships:

### Tottenham Hotspur (2020–2023)

- **Role:** Official Online Trading Partner

- **Significance:** Massive brand visibility across global audiences

### Sponsorship Strategy Analysis

Libertex's sponsorship portfolio spans **football (Premier League, Bundesliga)** and **motorsport (Formula 1)**, targeting:
- European and global audiences
- High-income demographics
- Male-skewed audiences who overlap with trading demographics
- Brand building in key European markets (UK, Germany)

This level of sponsorship investment suggests significant marketing budgets and a commitment to brand growth.

---

## 9. LIBERTEX INVEST — COMMISSION-FREE STOCK INVESTING

### Overview

Launched in **2024**, **Libertex Invest** is a significant product expansion allowing users to **purchase and hold actual stocks** — not CFDs — without commission fees.

### Key Features

- **Actual stock ownership** — not CFDs, you own the underlying shares
- **$0 commission** — no trading fees on stock purchases
- **Long-term holding** — designed for buy-and-hold investors
- **Dividends** — receive actual dividend payments
- **No overnight fees** — because you own the shares, no swap charges

### Strategic Significance

Libertex Invest represents a **fundamental shift** in Libertex's positioning:
- From **pure CFD broker** → **multi-product investment platform**
- Competes directly with DEGIRO, Trading 212, and eToro for stock investors
- Addresses the criticism that CFD-only platforms don't serve long-term investors
- May attract a new customer segment that avoids leveraged products

---

## 10. LBX — OFFSHORE BRAND (AUGUST 2025)

### Overview

In **August 2025**, Libertex Group launched **LBX**, a new offshore CFD brokerage brand operated by **MAEX Limited**, a company registered in **Mauritius**.

### Key Details

| Detail | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Brand | LBX |
| Operator | MAEX Limited |
| Jurisdiction | Mauritius (offshore) |
| Parent | Libertex Group |
| Launch | August 2025 |
| Purpose | Higher leverage, different regulatory framework |

### Implications

- **Higher leverage available** — offshore entities can offer 1:500+ vs. 1:30 EU maximum
- **Different regulatory standards** — Mauritius FSC vs. CySEC/FCA
- **Risk factor** — offshore regulation provides weaker investor protection
- **Brand confusion** — may muddy Libertex's "well-regulated" positioning
- **F1 deal connection** — LBX launch coincided with F1 sponsorship announcement

### Risk Assessment

The LBX offshore brand introduces a **moderate risk factor**. While the core Libertex brand remains well-regulated (CySEC/FCA/BaFin/FSCA), the offshore expansion suggests the group is pursuing higher-risk, higher-leverage markets. Investors should understand which entity they're trading with.

## 13. AFFILIATE / IB PROGRAM

### Status: AVAILABLE

Libertex operates an **IB (Introducing Broker) program**:

- Available for partners seeking to introduce clients
- Specific commission rates not publicly disclosed (contact Libertex for details)
- Standard IB structure typical of CySEC-regulated brokers
- Revenue sharing based on introduced client trading volume

### Compared to Forex Broker Affiliates

Libertex's IB program is less transparent than competitors who publish CPA ($200–$1,000+) or revenue share (20–50%) rates publicly. Interested partners should contact Libertex directly.

## 14. RISK ASSESSMENT — FULL ANALYSIS

### Positive Risk Factors

| Factor | Assessment | Impact |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| 4 strong regulators | ✅ CySEC/FCA/BaFin/FSCA | Low Risk |
| 29 years parent history | ✅ Extremely long track record | Low Risk |
| 700+ employees | ✅ Significant operational scale | Low Risk |
| Major sponsorships | ✅ Tottenham, Bayern, F1 | Low Risk |
| Negative balance protection | ✅ EU/UK mandatory | Low Risk |
| Clean current record | ✅ No active enforcement actions | Low Risk |
| MT4/MT5 availability | ✅ Industry-standard platforms | Low Risk |
| Libertex Invest | ✅ Stock ownership expansion | Low Risk |

### Negative Risk Factors

| Factor | Assessment | Impact |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| CySEC suspension (2021) | ⚠️ Brief partial suspension (resolved quickly) | Low-Medium Risk |
| Private ownership | ⚠️ No public financial transparency | Medium Risk |
| Offshore brand (LBX) | ⚠️ May dilute regulated brand trust | Medium Risk |
| Spread-only model | ⚠️ No transparency on execution quality | Low Risk |
| Limited instrument range | ⚠️ 300+ vs. thousands at competitors | Low Risk |
| No ECN accounts | ⚠️ Cannot verify interbank execution | Low Risk |

### Overall Risk Rating: **LOW-MEDIUM**

Libertex is a well-regulated, established CFD broker with a strong multi-jurisdiction license portfolio. The 29-year parent history, 700+ employees, and major sports sponsorships demonstrate operational scale and commitment. The main risks are: (1) private ownership limiting financial transparency, (2) the 2021 CySEC suspension (though quickly resolved), and (3) the offshore LBX brand expansion.

## 17. FINAL SCORING

### Detailed Scoring

| Category | Score | Max | Notes |
|----------|-------|-----|-------|
| **Regulation** | 8 | 10 | 4 strong regulators; minus for 2021 CySEC suspension |
| **Trading Conditions** | 6 | 10 | Spread-only 1.0–1.5; no ECN; limited instruments |
| **Platforms** | 7 | 10 | Proprietary + MT4/MT5; no cTrader/TradingView |
| **Deposits/Withdrawals** | 7 | 10 | Standard methods, $10 min deposit, $0 fees |
| **Customer Support** | 7 | 10 | Responsive per TrustPilot reviews |
| **Promotions** | 5 | 10 | Demo account, training; no deposit bonuses |
| **Education** | 5 | 10 | Basic training, demo account |
| **Awards** | 4 | 10 | Some recognition; no major identified awards |
| **Brand/Sponsorships** | 8 | 10 | Tottenham, Bayern, F1 — exceptional brand investment |
| **Financial Stability** | 6 | 10 | Private — no public financials; but large scale |

### Trader Type Ratings

| Trader Type | Rating | Verdict |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| Beginners | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | $10 min deposit, simple platform, demo account |
| EU/UK Traders | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Strong regulation, NBP, ICF/FSCS |
| MT4/MT5 Users | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Both platforms available |
| Long-Term CFD Holders | ⭐⭐⭐ | Spreads OK; swaps apply |
| Scalpers/Day Traders | ⭐⭐⭐ | Spreads OK but not tightest; no ECN |
| Stock Investors | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Libertex Invest — commission-free stocks |
| Algo Traders | ⭐⭐ | MT4/MT5 EAs only; no FIX API |
| Copy Traders | ⭐⭐ | Only MT4/MT5 signals |
| High-Leverage (EU) | ⭐ | 1:30 max under CySEC/FCA |
| High-Leverage (Offshore) | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | LBX offers higher leverage |

### Overall Assessment

**Libertex is a well-established, multi-regulated CFD broker with 29 years of parent history and major sports sponsorships (Tottenham, Bayern Munich, F1).** The spread-only pricing model from 1.0 pips is competitive for beginners, and the $10 minimum deposit makes it highly accessible. The 2024 launch of Libertex Invest (commission-free stock investing) and the MT4/MT5 availability broaden its appeal.

However, spreads are not the tightest (1.0–1.5 EUR/USD vs. 0.0–0.3 at ECN brokers), there's no cTrader or TradingView integration, and the private ownership means no public financial transparency. The CySEC suspension in 2021 (quickly resolved) and the 2025 offshore LBX brand expansion are moderate concerns.

For beginners and intermediate traders seeking a regulated, accessible CFD broker with a long track record and brand credibility, Libertex is a solid choice. For professional traders needing ultra-tight spreads, ECN execution, or advanced platform features, better options exist.
